channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korean police considering overseas travel ban on President Yoon over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean police said Monday they are considering placing an overseas travel ban on President Yoon Suk Yeol as they investigate possible rebellion charges over his brief imposition of martial law last week, reports said Monday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 men are standing trial on charges they chopped down Britain's scenic Sycamore Gap tree
item.description: LONDON (AP) — It was neither Britain’s biggest tree nor its oldest. But the majestic sycamore that stood for 150 years along a stretch of Hadrian’s Wall in northern England was one of the most beloved.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tonga's prime minister quits moments ahead of no-confidence motion in parliament
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Tonga’s Prime Minister Siaosi Sovaleni abruptly resigned in parliament on Monday ahead of a planned no-confidence vote in his leadership, capping a period of fraught relations between his government and Tonga’s king.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australian synagogue fire declared terrorism in a decision that increases investigation resources
item.description: Australian law enforcement authorities have declared an arson attack on a synagogue last week a terrorist act in a decision that increases resources available to the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Assad granted asylum in Russia, arrives in Moscow
item.description: Syrian President Bashar Assad and his family have been granted asylum in Russia and arrived in Moscow on Sunday after the Syrian government fell.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Ousted Syrian leader Assad flees to Moscow after fall of Damascus, Russian state media say
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Ousted Syrian leader Bashar Assad fled to Moscow and received asylum from his longtime ally, Russian media said Sunday, hours after a stunning rebel advance seized control of Damascus and ended his family’s 50 years of iron rule.
item.dc:creator: ABDULRAHMAN SHAHEEN, SARAH EL DEEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Global reaction to Assad's sudden ouster from Syria ranges from jubilation to alarm
item.description: Across the Middle East and beyond, the fall of Syria’s authoritarian government at the hands of jihadi militants set off waves of jubilation, trepidation and alarm.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump calls for immediate ceasefire in Ukraine and says a US withdrawal from NATO is possible
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump on Sunday pushed Russian leader Vladimir Putin to act to reach an immediate ceasefire with Ukraine, describing it as part of his active efforts as president-elect to end the war despite being weeks from taking office.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria latest: Syrians celebrate in the streets as Russian media says Assad has arrived in Moscow
item.description: Syrians poured into streets in celebration on Sunday after a stunning rebel advance reached the capital, ending the Assad family’s 50 years of iron rule. Russian state news agencies were reporting that President Bashar Assad and his family had arrived in Moscow and were given asylum.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How it happened: Two seismic weeks that toppled Syria's government
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Half a century of rule by the Assad family in Syria crumbled with astonishing speed after insurgents burst out of a rebel-held enclave and converged on the capital, Damascus, taking city after city in a matter of days.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gaza health officials say latest Israeli airstrikes kill at least 14 including children
item.description: DEIR AL BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes in central Gaza killed at least 14 people including children Sunday, Palestinian health officials said, while the bombing of a hospital in northern Gaza wounded a half-dozen patients.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turner says he believes there will be 'assessment as to whether' troops in Syria 'should' stay under Trump
item.description: House Intelligence Committee Chair Mike Turner (R-Ohio) said Sunday that under President-elect Trump, he believes there will “be an assessment as to whether or not” troops currently in Syria “should” stay.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: The fall of Bashar Assad after 13 years of war in Syria brings to an end a decades-long dynasty
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Syrian President Bashar Assad fled the country on Sunday, bringing to a dramatic close his nearly 14-year struggle to hold onto control as his country fragmented in a brutal civil war that became a proxy battlefield for regional and international powers.
item.dc:creator: ZEINA KARAM and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New reports claim UNRWA works with terrorists, teaches hate as agency hits back at critics
item.description: UNRWA is facing new allegations that its former and present leadership have met with Hamas and other terror-related groups. This comes as another NGO says agency-run schools continue to teach hate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump calls for 'immediate ceasefire' in Ukraine after meeting with Zelenskyy
item.description: In a post on his Truth Social platform, Trump claimed that Moscow and Kyiv have both lost hundreds of thousands of soldiers in a war that “should never have started.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Romania's far-right presidential candidate denounces canceled vote at closed polling station
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romania’s defiant far-right presidential candidate, Calin Georgescu, on Sunday stood outside a closed polling station to denounce a top court’s unprecedented decision to annul the first round of the vote in which he emerged as the frontrunner.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria latest: Syrians celebrate Bashar Assad's fall as his whereabouts remain unknown
item.description: Crowds gathered in Syria's Damascus on Sunday to celebrate the fall of Bashar Assad’s government with chants, prayers and the occasional gunfire after opposition fighters entered the capital following a stunning advance.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Russia abandoned Syria's Assad, never should have been involved
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said on Sunday that Russia's abandonment of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad led to his downfall.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Ghana's ex-President John Mahama is set to return after rival concedes election
item.description: Ghana’s former President John Dramani Mahama is set to return to office in the West African nation’s presidential election after the ruling party candidate conceded defeat.
item.dc:creator: Francis Kokutse And Chinedu Asadu, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korean prosecutors detain ex-defense chief over martial law imposition
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean prosecutors on Sunday detained a former defense minister who allegedly recommended last week’s brief but stunning martial law imposition to President Yoon Suk Yeol, making him the first figure detained over the case.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alone against a renewed insurgency, Assad may face the end of his rule without his strongest allies
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The last time Syrian President Bashar Assad was in serious trouble was 10 years ago, at the height of the country’s civil war, when his forces lost control over parts of the largest city, Aleppo, and his opponents were closing in on the capital, Damascus.
item.dc:creator: ZEINA KARAM and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syrian dictator Bashar Assad flees into exile as Islamist rebels conquer country
item.description: A lightning Syrian Islamist offensive over just days contributed to the demise of the over 50-year reign of the Assad Baathist regime in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrian insurgents reach gates of Damascus, threatening decades-long Assad regime
item.description: Syrian insurgents have reached the gates of the nation's capital, shortly after Bashar al-Assad's government abandoned the key city of Homs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's president avoids an impeachment attempt over short-lived martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's embattled President Yoon Suk Yeol avoided an opposition-led attempt to impeach him over his short-lived imposition of martial law, as most ruling party lawmakers boycotted a parliamentary vote Saturday to deny a two-thirds majority needed to suspend his presid
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump back on the world stage at Notre Dame reopening in Paris
item.description: Zelenskyy said his first face-to-face encounter with Trump since the election was “good and productive,” adding they had agreed to continue working together.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Assad regime in peril as insurgents near Syria's capital: What to know
item.description: Syrian rebels on Saturday battled government forces in an effort to take control of the crucial city of Homs as they advanced toward the country's capital, Damascus -- moves that catapulted President Bashar al-Assad's nearly 30 years of power into peril, Reuters reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Trump, Jill Biden attend Notre Dame reopening
item.description: President-elect Trump and first lady Jill Biden attended the reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris, sitting in the same row during the ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: South Korea's President Yoon survives impeachment attempt after his party boycotts vote
item.description: South Korean President Yoon survived an impeachment motion after members of his party boycotted the vote. Yoon attempted to impose martial law earlier this week, sparking backlash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump meets with Macron, Zelensky in France
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump visited with French President Emmanuel Macron on Saturday in Paris, for a meeting that he said was to discuss the world going "crazy.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Pope creates 21 cardinals, many of them reformers in their own right, to carry out his reform plans
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis on Saturday installed 21 new cardinals, many of whom are key figures in his reform agenda: A Dominican preacher who acted as the spiritual father for Francis' recent gathering of bishops, a Neapolitan “street priest” like himself, and a Peruvian bishop who has strongly backe
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An explosion destroys an apartment block in a Dutch city, killing at least 3 and injuring others
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — An explosion and fire rocked a neighborhood in the Dutch city of The Hague on Saturday, killing three people and injuring other people and destroying several apartments, according to authorities. The cause of the disaster was unclear.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean president apologizes for declaring martial law ahead of impeachment vote
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol apologized for declaring martial law earlier in the week, as he now prepares for a parliamentary vote on his impeachment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran launches rocket with heaviest-ever payload into space amid heightened concern over nuclear program
item.description: Iran launches rocket into space carrying its heaviest-ever payload, as security experts remain concerned by its "drastically" increased stockpiles of enriched uranium near nuclear weapons grade purity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with Hungary’s effort to end Holocaust survivor lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday probed the international consequences of allowing a group of Holocaust survivors and their heirs to leverage U.S. courts to seek compensation from Hungary for confiscating their property.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 11 in Lebanon after fire exchanges with Hezbollah that test ceasefire’s limits
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel unleashed its largest wave of airstrikes across Lebanon since agreeing to a ceasefire with Hezbollah last week, killing at least 11 people on Monday after the Lebanese militant group fired a volley of projectiles as a warning over what it said were Israeli truce violations.
item.dc:creator: NATALIE MELTZER and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation
